steps.title: Use the condition for infinitely many solutions
steps.content: For a linear equation to have infinitely many solutions, the two sides must simplify to exactly the same expression for every value of $x$.

steps.title: Confirm with coefficients
steps.content: Expanding gives $$18(x+6)=18x+108$$ and $$2(x+c)+16x=18x+2c.$$ The $x$-coefficients are already both $18$, so only the constants need to match: $$108=2c$$ $$c=54$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: The value $c=54$ matches Choice B — $54$. Substituting $c=54$ gives $18x+108=18x+108$, an identity, so the equation has infinitely many solutions.
